keyPoints: Residuals should have no autocorrelation (use ACF/PACF plots and Ljung-Box test).
Residuals should be approximately normally distributed (use Q-Q plot or Shapiro-Wilk test).
Residuals should have constant variance (homoscedasticity), checked via a time series plot or tests like ARCH.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for residual autocorrelation, leading to a misspecified model.
Assuming normality without formal testing, especially with small sample sizes.
Overlooking changing variance (heteroscedasticity), which invalidates many model assumptions.
